messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following veterinary practice privacy notice for GDPR compliance:

"PawCare Vets collects your name, contact details, and payment information to manage your account and process invoices. We also hold records of your pet's treatment history. Invoices and payment records are retained for 7 years for tax purposes. You may update your contact details by calling reception. We use VetSoft practice management software under a data processing agreement."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PARTIAL] Legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c)) implied for tax records and contract (Art. 6(1)(b)) for service delivery, but neither is explicitly stated.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PASS] Not applicable. Pet treatment data does not constitute personal special category data — the data subject is the owner, not the pet.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Account management, invoicing, and tax compliance are stated as purposes.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Name, contact details, payment information, and pet treatment history are proportionate to the stated purposes.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] 7-year retention for invoices and payment records stated. Retention for contact details and pet treatment history is not addressed.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Only update of contact details (rectification) referenced. Other rights absent.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] VetSoft named as practice management software provider under a DPA.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [FAIL] Not addressed. Practice management software providers may use non-EEA infrastructure.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not addressed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Non-Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (lawful basis not explicitly stated), Art. 5 (contact details and treatment history retention absent), Art. 15-21 (most rights absent), Art. 46 (transfer safeguards not addressed), Art. 37 (DPO not addressed), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Explicitly state lawful basis for each processing activity
- Add retention periods for contact details and pet treatment history
- Add a full data subject rights section with contact instructions
- Address international transfer safeguards for VetSoft if applicable
- Confirm DPO requirement and disclose if applicable
- Add breach notification commitment
